# kolm · your own AI, compiled to your task

> One signed file. Runs anywhere. Receipts on every call.
> kolm is the AI compiler. Take a task spec, compile it into a `.kolm` artifact:
> a single signed file containing recipes, a small LoRA, a verifier, an index,
> and tests. Run it offline on a phone, edge box, or laptop. Every inference
> emits a verifiable receipt anchored in a four-ring HMAC chain.

## Core idea

Frontier models are general. Production tasks are specific. Most tokens you
spend with Anthropic and OpenAI are wasted on capability you do not use.

kolm captures (input, verified-output) pairs from the frontier API calls you
already make, distills them into a task-specific local LoRA, and signs the
artifact. The compute you rent compounds into compute you own.

## What is a `.kolm` file?

A signed zip with seven segments: model.gguf · lora.bin · recipes.json ·
index.sqlite-vec · verifier.json · manifest.json · signature.sig.
Specified by RS-1 (MIT-licensed). In v0.1 the slots hold: a JSON base-model
pointer (model.gguf), an optional KOLMPACK behavior pack (lora.bin), and an
optional KOLMIDX JSON lookup (index.sqlite-vec). Native quantized weights,
GGUF-LoRA adapters, and sqlite-vec databases are roadmap tiers. Receipts are
HMAC-SHA256; the issuer or holder of the shared tenant receipt secret can
re-verify offline. Ed25519 public-key receipts and Sigstore Rekor anchoring
are roadmap.

## Primary URLs

- https://kolm.ai/ · landing page
- https://kolm.ai/manifesto · why we built this
- https://kolm.ai/how-it-works · the compile loop
- https://kolm.ai/anatomy · `.kolm` byte-map
- https://kolm.ai/k-score · the per-artifact K-score gate, formula and thresholds (each .kolm has its own score, not the base model)
- https://kolm.ai/compile · the compile step
- https://kolm.ai/serve · local MCP endpoint
- https://kolm.ai/run · on-device inference
- https://kolm.ai/recall · recipe registry
- https://kolm.ai/pricing · six-tier ladder, BYO frontier key
- https://kolm.ai/benchmarks · reproducible runs, public seeds
- https://kolm.ai/whitepaper · RS-1 specification, full text
- https://kolm.ai/security · threat model and mitigations
- https://kolm.ai/trust · receipt walker, K-score gates
- https://kolm.ai/api · endpoint reference
- https://kolm.ai/docs · quickstart and SDK reference
- https://kolm.ai/articles · long-form essays
- https://kolm.ai/use-cases · six built-out patterns
- https://kolm.ai/cookbook · recipe examples
- https://kolm.ai/roadmap · what shipped and what is queued, version-by-version

## Use-cases

- https://kolm.ai/use-cases/agentic-coding · captured-pair distillation for coding agents
- https://kolm.ai/use-cases/capture-and-distill · rent-vs-buy compute loop
- https://kolm.ai/use-cases/ai-saas · per-tenant LoRA, BYOK, gross margin
- https://kolm.ai/use-cases/mobile · on-device, offline, signed
- https://kolm.ai/use-cases/embedded · edge boxes, MCUs, isolated networks
- https://kolm.ai/use-cases/web3-verifiable · Future cryptographic anchoring

## Key concepts

- `.kolm` (artifact) · the signed binary; one file you ship and run
- RS-1 (specification) · the open spec; MIT for code, CC-BY for text
- K-score (quality gate, per-artifact) · `K = 0.40·A + 0.15·S + 0.15·L + 0.15·C + 0.15·V`. Computed for each compiled .kolm against its own frozen eval set; NOT a leaderboard for the underlying base model.
- A (accuracy) · pass rate on held-out positives
- S (size score) · inverse of artifact bytes on disk
- L (latency score) · inverse of p50 inference latency
- C (cost score) · inverse of training + inference cost
- V (coverage) · fraction of intents the recipe pack resolves
- four-ring receipt chain · pre-image → derived → execution → seal, HMAC-SHA256

## Proof points (verifiable)

- SWE-bench Lite harness: ships at `kolm bench --reproduce swebench-lite-n150`
  against the official swebench 4.1.0 evaluator, seed=42, Opus-4.7. The
  headline lift lands when the first end-to-end signed run completes; no
  point estimate is published before then.
- Smoke battery: 792/0 tests, runs against any URL via
  `URL=https://kolm.ai bash scripts/smoke-live.sh`.
- Receipts: Sigstore Rekor anchoring is opt-in, off by default.

## Pricing

Six tiers. Free CLI ($0). Mobile ($9). Pro ($49). Team ($149). Business
($1,499). Enterprise ($2,999). All tiers BYO frontier-API key. We never resell
tokens. The customer's spend stays the customer's spend; we charge for the
artifact, not the inference.

## Brand

- Kolmogorov · the company
- kolm · the binary (the CLI)
- `.kolm` · the file format
- RS-1 · the specification (MIT-licensed)

Inspired by Andrey Kolmogorov's 1965 question: what is the smallest specialist
program that produces a given behavior? The compiler answers it for any task.

## Repository

- https://github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack · server + CLI + spec
- install: `npm i -g github:sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack` (source-install; `kolm` lands on PATH)
- license: MIT (code) · CC-BY-4.0 (text)

- general: see https://kolm.ai/contact
